# Unity UI Toolkit Design System

> A drop-in design system for Unity 6 UI Toolkit (UIDocument and PanelRenderer, UXML and USS). Design tokens, 42 components, 120 SVG icons, a Google Fonts typography system with real weights and multilingual fallbacks, a one-class mobile flip, and a small auto-attaching C# runtime, all themed dark and editable from a single stylesheet. Battle-tested in the shipping cross-platform game Leap of Legends.

Package id `com.sinanata.designsystem`, MIT licensed. Needs Unity 6000.0 or newer; the `PanelRenderer` backend, used for the showcase's world-space gallery, needs 6000.5+ (both `UIDocument` and `PanelRenderer` can host flat or world-space UI). You style UI by composing `ds-`-prefixed BEM classes and `var(--...)` design tokens; the C# runtime auto-attaches and needs no wiring. The canonical class list is `docs/COMPONENTS.md`.

Typography: `Design System > Google Fonts` imports any of 2045 families. Most ship as a single variable font, which Unity's own Font Asset Creator can only open at one weight; the importer reads the `fvar` table and extracts every named instance, so one file yields the full 100-900 ramp with real outlines. It wires each face's `fontWeightTable`, which makes every existing `-unity-font-style: bold` rule resolve to a real Bold rather than a synthetically dilated Regular. For multilingual text, a fallback chain is mandatory, not optional: with none configured Unity quietly borrows an OS font, so Arabic and CJK render correctly in the Editor and as empty boxes in a WebGL build. A chain alone still cannot tell Chinese from Japanese, because they share codepoints they draw differently and a chain resolves per codepoint rather than per language, so name the face with `DsFonts.ApplyFace` when you know the locale. Shaping and right-to-left (Arabic joining, Devanagari reordering) live in Unity's advanced text generator, which `DsFonts` selects per line from the content; and because a downloaded font can be shaped too (`DsFonts.TryEnableShaping`), any of the 2045 families can be fetched live in a browser and serve every language it covers, joined. See `docs/FONTS.md`.

## Documentation
- [README](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/README.md): what it is, three install paths, quick start, requirements, architecture overview, live showcase.
- [Component reference](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/docs/COMPONENTS.md): canonical list of every `ds-*` class with its DOM and states.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): the USS stack, tokens, load-bearing import order, the runtime layer, theming, and the mobile pattern.
- [Fonts](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/docs/FONTS.md): the Google Fonts importer, variable-font weight extraction, real vs synthetic bold, the multilingual fallback chain and why its order is load-bearing, the advanced text generator, and runtime font downloading.
- [Icons](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/docs/ICONS.md): the 120 white-fill SVGs, the tint cascade, and how to add one.
- [Mobile](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/docs/MOBILE.md): the one-class `.mobile` responsive flip.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): naming convention, file-load order, and PR checklist.
- [Agent guide (AGENTS.md)](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/AGENTS.md): rules and commands for AI coding agents.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): version history in Keep a Changelog format.

## Source of truth (read these to use classes correctly)
- [DesignTokens.uss](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/Assets/DesignSystem/Resources/UI/Styles/DesignSystem/DesignTokens.uss): every color, spacing, radius, and motion token.
- [DesignSystem.uss](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/Assets/DesignSystem/Resources/UI/Styles/DesignSystem/DesignSystem.uss): the master stylesheet that `@import`s the layers in order.
- [DesignSystemShowcase.uxml](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/Assets/DesignSystem/Resources/UI/Styles/DesignSystem/DesignSystemShowcase.uxml): every class rendered in at least one state, with its expected DOM.
- [DesignSystemRuntimeBase.cs](https://github.com/sinanata/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/blob/main/Assets/DesignSystem/Runtime/DesignSystemRuntimeBase.cs): the auto-attaching runtime (toggle knobs, spinner rotation, skeleton shimmer, drag and drop).

## Live demo
- [Interactive web showcase](https://sinanata.github.io/unity-ui-toolkit-design-system/): all 36 sections, flat and world-space, day and night, click a component to copy its class list. Fetch any Google Font live, walk the 100-900 weight ramp, and read a language-availability table across seventeen scripts including Arabic and Hebrew right-to-left.

## Optional
- [Leap of Legends](https://leapoflegends.com): the shipping cross-platform game this system was built for and is battle-tested in.
